Title: A plant NLR receptor activates auxin signaling through Aux/IAAs-ARF19 and YUC8-TIR1/AFBs to promote callose-mediated antiviral defense

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NLR immune receptor with N-terminal Solanaceae domain (SD)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and nucleus; SD required for nuclear entry
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NSm; NbIAA35; SlIAA35; NbIAA13; NbIAA27; NbIAA29; NbIAA33
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Callose blocks viral movement; IAA amplifies defense signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates auxin signaling and callose deposition to restrict TSWV intercellular movement and accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in N. benthamiana; activated upon NSm recognition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Recognition of TSWV movement protein NSm
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major resistance gene for TSWV; its mechanism suggests stacking with auxin/callose modules could improve antiviral durability
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Table S1), qRT-PCR, LC-MS/MS, interaction assays, infection assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sw-5b recognition of NSm initiates plasmodesmal callose deposition and auxin signaling. 2. It relieves Aux/IAA repression on ARF19 and activates the YUC8-TIR1/AFB amplification circuit. 3. This mechanism confines TSWV by strengthening callose-mediated barriers.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; reporter movement assay; RNA-seq; Co-IP; BiFC; Y2H/Y3H; callose staining; LC-MS/MS; virus inoculation assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary immune receptor and central subject of the paper, but not a pathway enzyme or classical transcriptional regulator; categorized here as Common_Gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes defense-associated auxin signaling and callose deposition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upon AVR8 recognition, activates auxin-responsive genes, increases IAA content, elevates GSL8 expression, and induces callose deposition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Recognition of Phytophthora infestans effector AVR8
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests SD-containing NLRs may be broadly exploitable to couple pathogen recognition with auxin/callose defense outputs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. R8 SD interacts with NbIAA13 and NbIAA27. 2. Activated R8 induces auxin signaling and IAA accumulation. 3. R8 also promotes GSL8 expression and callose deposition, suggesting a conserved SD-NLR mechanism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; transient coexpression with AVR8; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS; aniline blue staining
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a secondary experimentally tested NLR extending the mechanism beyond Sw-5b.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into callose deposited at plasmodesmata
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Restricts plasmodesmal permeability and viral cell-to-cell movement; contributes to antiviral defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes plasmodesmal callose deposition and enhances resistance to TSWV; silencing weakens Sw-5b-mediated movement restriction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in leaves upon Sw-5b activation by NSm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV NSm recognition by Sw-5b; TSWV infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance callose-based antiviral barriers, but caution due to growth/development defects when GSL5/8/10 are jointly reduced
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Joint knockdown with GSL8/10 caused severe developmental defects, indicating growth-related tradeoffs in manipulating this module
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Table S1), qRT-PCR, transient overexpression, RNAi silencing, virus infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGSL5 was strongly up-regulated after Sw-5b activation by NSm. 2. Overexpression induced callose deposition and reduced TSWV infection. 3. Joint silencing with NbGSL8/10 reduced callose and compromised Sw-5b-mediated antiviral defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; transient overexpression; dsRNA-mediated RNAi cosilencing; aniline blue staining; TSWV infectious clone assay; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional evidence in this paper mainly derives from combined GSL5/8/10 analyses rather than a single-gene knockout.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into callose deposited at plasmodesmata
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Restricts plasmodesmal permeability and viral cell-to-cell movement; contributes to antiviral defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct ARF19 target whose activation increases callose deposition and host resistance to TSWV; impaired in arf19 plants and upon GSL5/8/10 silencing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly up-regulated in leaves upon Sw-5b activation by NSm; induced by auxin and ARF19
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV NSm recognition by Sw-5b; exogenous IAA; TSWV infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering antiviral resistance via enhanced plasmodesmal callose deposition, but likely requires balanced regulation to avoid developmental costs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of the GSL5/8/10 module affects development; callose overaccumulation may impair intercellular transport
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Table S1), qRT-PCR, luciferase assay, EMSA, CRISPR mutant analysis, RNAi silencing, metabolite/callose phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGSL8 is strongly induced by Sw-5b activation and by auxin. 2. NbARF19 directly binds the NbGSL8 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. NbGSL8 contributes to plasmodesmal callose deposition that restricts TSWV spread.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; CRISPR-Cas9 arf19 mutant test of target induction; overexpression; dsRNA-mediated RNAi; aniline blue staining; virus assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among GSL genes, NbGSL8 received the strongest direct mechanistic support as an ARF19 transcriptional target.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into callose deposited at plasmodesmata
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Restricts plasmodesmal permeability and viral cell-to-cell movement; contributes to antiviral defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes callose deposition and antiviral resistance to TSWV; part of the Sw-5b-induced GSL5/8/10 module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in leaves upon Sw-5b activation by NSm; induced by auxin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV NSm recognition by Sw-5b; exogenous IAA; TSWV infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen callose-based antiviral defense, with caution about growth defects from perturbing combined GSL function
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Joint knockdown with GSL5/8 produced severe developmental abnormalities
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Table S1), qRT-PCR, transient overexpression, RNAi silencing, virus infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGSL10 is induced by Sw-5b activation and auxin. 2. Overexpression elevates callose deposition and restricts TSWV infection. 3. Joint silencing with NbGSL5/8 reduces callose accumulation and compromises defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; transient overexpression; dsRNA-mediated RNAi cosilencing; aniline blue staining; TSWV infectious clone assay; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike NbGSL8, direct transcriptional activation by ARF19 was not explicitly demonstrated.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the YUCCA monooxygenase step in the indole-3-pyruvic acid pathway leading to auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amplifies auxin signaling to promote callose deposition and antiviral defense against TSWV
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA accumulation, auxin-responsive gene expression, callose deposition, and resistance to TSWV; essential for Sw-5b-mediated auxin amplification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically up-regulated in leaves upon Sw-5b activation by NSm; induced by ARF19
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV NSm recognition by Sw-5b; TSWV infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to boost auxin-dependent antiviral defense; may be useful in stacking with ARF19/GSL modules for durable resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotype in NbYUC8-silenced plants in this study, but auxin pathway manipulation may have broader developmental effects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Table S1), LC-MS/MS for IAA, qRT-PCR, overexpression, TRV silencing, EMSA, arf19 mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbYUC8 is the only YUC gene strongly induced by Sw-5b activation. 2. Silencing NbYUC8 reduced Sw-5b-induced IAA accumulation by ~90% and attenuated auxin signaling. 3. NbARF19 directly activates NbYUC8, and NbYUC8 promotes callose deposition and antiviral defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; LC-MS/MS; qRT-PCR; overexpression; TRV-VIGS; EMSA; arf19 mutant analysis; TSWV infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly frames YUC enzymes as catalyzing a rate-limiting step in auxin biosynthesis; only NbYUC8 was positively induced by Sw-5b.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor (ARF) with DNA-binding domain and PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NbIAA35; NbIAA13; NbIAA27; NbIAA29; NbIAA33; Sw-5b SD (indirect competition context)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Callose biosynthesis and auxin biosynthesis/signaling amplification
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf plasmodesmata for callose; leaf tissues for IAA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Callose restricts viral movement; IAA amplifies defense-associated auxin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of Sw-5b-mediated callose deposition, auxin biosynthesis, and resistance to TSWV
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active in leaf tissues; overexpression in leaves increases NbGSL8 and NbYUC8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TSWV NSm recognition by Sw-5b; TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Release from Aux/IAA repression upon Sw-5b activation; amplified by auxin/TIR1-AFB signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in positive feedforward amplification via ARF19→YUC8→IAA→TIR1/AFBs→Aux/IAA degradation→more ARF19 activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in Aux/IAA-ARF transcriptional repression/activation module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator linking NLR signaling to both callose synthesis and auxin amplification
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering node for antiviral resistance by coordinating both structural barrier formation and hormone amplification
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: arf19 mutants show leaf curling; pathway manipulation may affect development
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context, qRT-PCR, dual-luciferase, EMSA, CRISPR-Cas9 mutant, RNAi/VIGS, LC-MS/MS, virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbARF19 localizes to the nucleus and interacts with Aux/IAA repressors. 2. It directly binds and activates NbGSL8 and NbYUC8 promoters. 3. Loss of ARF19 compromises Sw-5b-induced callose deposition, auxin amplification, and TSWV resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; confocal localization; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RNAi/VIGS; callose staining; virus accumulation assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NbARF7, despite prior analogy to Arabidopsis ARF7, was not functionally supported in this leaf antiviral context because it localized mainly in the cytoplasm.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulation of antiviral auxin/callose defense inferred from silencing phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Restricts TSWV spread and supports antiviral immunity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing SlARF19 increases TSWV accumulation and HR lesion size in tomato carrying Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Aux/IAA repression and Sw-5b-mediated defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved positive regulator of Sw-5b-mediated antiviral defense in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving TSWV resistance in tomato germplasm carrying Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlARF19 is required for robust resistance to TSWV in tomato. 2. Silencing SlARF19 causes more severe disease and higher viral accumulation. 3. It supports conserved Sw-5b-mediated auxin/callose defense across species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; TSWV inoculation; symptom observation; immunoblot/qRT-PCR of viral accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter-binding evidence was shown for NbARF19, while tomato evidence was mainly genetic/phenotypic.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aux/IAA family protein with conserved Aux/IAA domains including PB1 interaction region
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF19 activity, thereby reducing NbGSL8 transcription and callose accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via ARF sequestration; auxin-induced proteolysis through TIR1/AFBs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling gating of callose biosynthesis and auxin amplification
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plasmodesmal closure and antiviral defense through repression of auxin-responsive outputs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator that suppresses callose deposition and promotes TSWV infection when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: IAA via TIR1/AFB receptors; Sw-5b activation through NSm recognition
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of positive amplification loop because auxin promotes TIR1/AFB-mediated degradation of IAA repressors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent repressor relieved by Sw-5b and auxin signaling to enable antiviral callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing IAA35-like repression could strengthen antiviral defense, but broad auxin-pathway effects require caution
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating Aux/IAA repressors may affect development and auxin-regulated growth
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H screen; BiFC; Co-IP; luciferase assay; RNAi; virus assays; western blot degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbIAA35 was identified as an Sw-5b SD-interacting Aux/IAA repressor. 2. It represses callose accumulation and favors TSWV infection. 3. Sw-5b and TIR1/AFBs relieve its repression by interfering with ARF19 binding and promoting auxin-dependent turnover.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H library screening; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; luciferase assay; RNAi; overexpression; competitive interaction assays; degradation assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-gene silencing had limited phenotype because of redundancy with other Aux/IAAs.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF19-dependent activation of callose/auxin defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling repression upstream of callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with IAA27/29/33/35, negatively regulates callose deposition and promotes TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant repressor within the decisive Aux/IAA set targeted by Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Combined attenuation of this repressor set may enhance antiviral resistance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely developmental impacts if constitutively perturbed
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; luciferase; callose and virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbIAA13 interacts with Sw-5b SD and NbARF19. 2. It acts redundantly with other Aux/IAAs to repress callose-associated defense genes. 3. Combined silencing of the Aux/IAA set enhances callose and restricts TSWV.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; dual-luciferase; aniline blue staining; virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional evidence is as part of the combined IAA13/27/29/33/35 module rather than as an isolated gene.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF19-dependent activation of defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling repression upstream of callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of redundant Aux/IAA repressor set suppressing callose deposition and favoring TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant but important repressor targeted by Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multigene editing strategies to enhance antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely developmental effects if broadly deregulated
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; luciferase; callose and virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbIAA27 binds Sw-5b SD and NbARF19. 2. It contributes redundantly to repression of callose-associated antiviral defense. 3. Derepression of this module enhances resistance to TSWV.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; dual-luciferase; aniline blue staining; virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence mainly from combined Aux/IAA silencing module.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF19-dependent activation of defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling repression upstream of callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of redundant Aux/IAA repressor set suppressing callose deposition and favoring TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant but important repressor targeted by Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multigene editing strategies to enhance antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely developmental effects if broadly deregulated
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; luciferase; callose and virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbIAA29 interacts with Sw-5b SD and NbARF19. 2. It functions redundantly with related Aux/IAAs to repress callose-associated immunity. 3. Joint silencing enhances antiviral defense.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; dual-luciferase; aniline blue staining; virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence mainly from combined Aux/IAA silencing module.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ARF19-dependent activation of defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling repression upstream of callose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of redundant Aux/IAA repressor set suppressing callose deposition and favoring TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant but important repressor targeted by Sw-5b
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multigene editing strategies to enhance antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely developmental effects if broadly deregulated
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; luciferase; callose and virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbIAA33 interacts with Sw-5b SD and NbARF19. 2. It redundantly suppresses ARF19-mediated callose defense. 3. Relief of this repression contributes to Sw-5b antiviral immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Y3H; RNAi cosilencing; dual-luciferase; aniline blue staining; virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence mainly from combined Aux/IAA silencing module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TIR1/AFB auxin receptor family; F-box proteins functioning in SCF ubiquitin ligase complexes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of Aux/IAA repressors, indirectly enhancing ARF19-dependent defense gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling amplification upstream of callose biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amplifies defense-associated auxin output leading to stronger callose barriers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full Sw-5b-induced auxin signaling, callose deposition, and antiviral defense against TSWV
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous IAA; Sw-5b activation by NSm; TSWV infection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Elevated IAA generated via YUC8 after Sw-5b activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Central component of positive amplification loop downstream of YUC8-derived auxin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential amplifier of the auxin-defense circuit rather than the initial trigger
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen hormone-amplified antiviral immunity, but receptor manipulation may strongly affect growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing in tomato caused stunted growth; broad auxin pathway perturbation has developmental costs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic analysis; RNAi/VIGS; qRT-PCR; western blot degradation assay; callose staining; virus assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbTIR1/AFBs mediate auxin-induced degradation of NbIAA35. 2. Silencing attenuates Sw-5b-induced auxin-responsive gene expression and callose deposition. 3. They are required for full antiviral defense against TSWV.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLAST/phylogeny; dsRNA-mediated RNAi; IAA-induced degradation assay; qRT-PCR; aniline blue staining; virus infection assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper functionally analyzed a combined silencing set including NbTIR1A/B, AFB4, and AFB6 rather than resolving each member individually.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin signaling gating in tomato antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tomato ortholog that interacts with Sw-5b and participates in the conserved Aux/IAA repression module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports conservation of the Sw-5b-targeted repression mechanism in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target in tomato improvement when combined with Sw-5b-mediated resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlIAA35 physically interacts with Sw-5b/SD and SlARF19. 2. These interactions support a conserved repression-release mechanism in tomato. 3. Sw-5b can competitively interfere with SlIAA35-ARF19 association.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; GST pull-down; Y3H
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato phenotypic evidence centered more strongly on SlARF19 and SlTIR1/AFBs than on SlIAA35 itself.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TIR1/AFB auxin receptor family; F-box proteins in SCF complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes ARF-dependent defense gene expression by degrading Aux/IAA repressors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for strong Sw-5b-mediated antiviral defense in tomato; silencing enlarges HR foci and elevates TSWV N levels
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: IAA elevation downstream of YUC8/auxin biosynthesis module
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of positive auxin amplification circuit
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential amplifier of Sw-5b-associated defense in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway for strengthening TSWV resistance, but growth penalties are a concern
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: VIGS silencing caused stunted growth in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlTIR1/AFBs are required for robust Sw-5b-mediated defense in tomato. 2. Silencing increases HR lesion size and viral RNA accumulation. 3. The auxin receptor amplification module is conserved across Nicotiana and tomato.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; TSWV inoculation; lesion measurement; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed as a combined family set, not individual receptors.
